task_completion.description: Verify that the agent has opened the chart for patient Walter Bishop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient header or active context in the NOSH ChartingSystem shows 'Walter Bishop', '01/20/1948', or PID '9999'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent has navigated to the letter creation section or opened a new letter form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form, modal, or page for creating a new letter or communication is visible within the patient's chart.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct subject is entered for the letter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Insurance Claim Denial - Action Required' is visible in the subject or title input field of the letter form.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct content is entered in the letter body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text containing 'missing coordination of benefits' is visible in the main text area or body of the letter form.

task_completion.description: Verify that the letter has been saved or added to the patient's chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification (e.g., 'Letter saved'), the letter appearing in a list of patient documents/communications, or a view of the finalized letter.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser to interact with the NOSH ChartingSystem.
integrity.visual_evidence: The NOSH ChartingSystem is visible in a web browser, and actions are performed through its UI.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands or database scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows should not show SQL commands or scripts being executed to insert the letter directly into the database.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just type the letter in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The letter content and subject must be entered into the NOSH web application's specific fields, not a generic text editor like gedit or nano.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient: Walter Bishop (PID: 9999). Expected Subject: 'Insurance Claim Denial - Action Required'. Expected Content must include the fragment 'missing coordination of benefits'.
